brd.upstream: specs/brd/source-frd.md (FRD mode) or specs/brd/interview-requirements.json (interview mode); null only for pre-spine legacy projects
brd.hard_gate: FRD mode: specs/reviews/brd-grounding.json must have pass=true. Any net_new (BRD requirement invented relative to the FRD + clarification log) or any dropped (FRD requirement no BRD requirement covers) is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average — the same hard-gate semantics as the security verdict. The grounding check is deterministic (grounding-check.js over the *-requirements.json files), so the evaluator confirms its verdict rather than re-judging traceability by reading prose. Interview-from-scratch mode: the same gate runs against specs/brd/interview-requirements.json (the confirmed INT-n interview spine) — identical net_new/dropped semantics and the same verdict file; the gate is skipped only when neither requirements spine exists. Additionally, specs/reviews/brd-taxonomy.json must have pass=true: every one of the ten requirement-taxonomy slots (functional, data_lifecycle, integration, performance, security_authz, privacy_retention, observability, operability_failure, ux_accessibility, constraints) is either covered by a tagged BR requirement or excused with a substantive na_reason in taxonomy-coverage.json. This is the check grounding structurally cannot make — grounding is relative to a source document that may itself be silent on retention, authz, or failure modes.

criteria.completeness: All BRD sections present with substantive content (not placeholder text), including the BRD Analysis Summary. FRD mode: every FRD-n requirement in frd-requirements.json is covered by >= 1 BRD requirement (zero dropped). Interview mode: every INT-n in interview-requirements.json is covered by >= 1 BRD requirement (zero dropped). specs/brd/brd-analysis.json exists and contains domain concepts, ambiguity, edge-case, decision, acceptance criteria coverage, and risk/gap entries. Comprehensiveness is anchored to specs/reviews/brd-taxonomy.json (zero uncovered, zero unjustified slots), not judged from prose. specs/brd/brd-acceptance.json splits each requirement postcondition into individually traceable BR-n-ACm ids, and specs/brd/brd-safeguards.json records invariants/prohibitions/limits/norms
criteria.traceability: FRD mode: every BRD requirement in brd-requirements.json traces to an FRD section id or a C-n clarification; zero net-new (invented) requirements — anchored to specs/reviews/brd-grounding.json, not free judgement. Interview-from-scratch mode: every BRD requirement traces to an INT-n interview requirement or a C-n clarification — anchored to the same specs/reviews/brd-grounding.json verdict
criteria.specificity: >= 3 quantified success metrics with numeric targets; no subjective language (avoid 'fast', 'easy', 'intuitive' without measurable definition). Ambiguity table entries name a default assumption, risk-if-wrong, resolution, and trace
criteria.consistency: Scope, MVP definition, architecture section, domain concepts, edge-case table, and risk/gap table are mutually aligned with no contradictions
criteria.actionability: MVP feature set maps directly to stated scope; >= 2 alternatives documented with trade-off rationale; acceptance criteria coverage matrix maps every source requirement to observable ACs with no unexplained gaps

spec.hard_gate: When specs/brd/brd-requirements.json exists, specs/reviews/spec-grounding.json (from trace-check.js over story-traces.json) must have pass=true. Any net_new (story tracing to no BRD requirement) or dropped (BRD requirement no story realizes) is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average — same semantics as the security verdict. The check is deterministic, so the evaluator confirms it rather than re-judging traceability from prose. Step 4.5 additionally requires story-clusters.js to exit 0 — no interface contract may have contract_story: null, since that means the consumer must wait for the producer full implementation and the clusters are not independently ownable. When specs/brd/brd-acceptance.json exists, specs/reviews/spec-acceptance-grounding.json must also pass: every BRD acceptance postcondition is realized by at least one story acceptance criterion.

criteria.completeness: Every BRD requirement has >= 1 story; no requirement left uncovered (zero dropped in spec-grounding.json when present)
criteria.traceability: Every story in story-traces.json carries >= 1 BR-n trace; zero net-new stories — anchored to specs/reviews/spec-grounding.json when present, else every story references a BRD goal with zero orphans
criteria.specificity: Acceptance criteria use observable outcomes (HTTP status codes, numeric values, measurable thresholds); reject vague language ('should work', 'handles errors'). Every acceptance criterion is a well-formed Given/When/Then object with a stable {story}-AC{n} id and an observable then-clause that does not merely restate the when-clause
criteria.consistency: Story dependency graph is acyclic; layer assignments (frontend/backend/infra) are valid and non-contradictory
criteria.actionability: All stories are implementation-ready with 3-6 acceptance criteria, groups assigned, story points on the 1/2/3/5/8/13 scale, estimation confidence and drivers, every AC maps to a features.json entry, and every AC has a stable {story}-AC{n} id in story-traces.json, every dependency is typed (contract|data|behavior|ui) with an artifact and reason, every story carries business_value/scope_in/scope_out and an INVEST scorecard whose independent letter is backfilled from story-clusters.json#independently_startable, and specs/stories/story-clusters.json exists with zero unresolved interface contracts

test.hard_gate: When specs/stories/story-traces.json exists, specs/reviews/test-grounding.json (trace-check.js over test-traces.json vs the AC index) must have pass=true. Any net_new (test case tracing to no AC) or dropped (AC with no test case — an untested requirement) is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average.

criteria.completeness: Every acceptance criterion has >= 1 test case (zero dropped in test-grounding.json when present); success, error, and boundary paths covered
criteria.traceability: Every test case in test-traces.json traces to a {story}-AC{n} id; zero net-new test cases — anchored to specs/reviews/test-grounding.json when present
criteria.specificity: Test cases assert observable outcomes (status codes, exact values, error types), not 'works correctly'; fixtures use domain-representative data, never 'foo'/'test'/123
criteria.consistency: Test data fixtures match the data-model schemas; test case ids are unique; AC references resolve to real story acceptance criteria
criteria.actionability: Each test case has preconditions, ordered steps, and an expected result an evaluator can execute

design.hard_gate: When specs/stories/story-traces.json exists, specs/reviews/design-grounding.json (trace-check.js over design-traces.json vs the stories) must have pass=true. Any net_new (component realizing no story) or dropped (story no component realizes) is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average.

criteria.completeness: Component-map covers every story; UI stories have mockups, API stories have endpoint definitions (zero dropped stories in design-grounding.json when present)
criteria.traceability: Every component in design-traces.json traces to >= 1 story; zero net-new components — anchored to specs/reviews/design-grounding.json when present, else every story ID appears in component-map and API endpoints trace to acceptance criteria
criteria.specificity: OpenAPI specs and JSON Schemas are syntactically valid; entity definitions are cross-referenced across components
criteria.consistency: Mockup form fields match API request/response schemas; no port conflicts between services; each source file owned by exactly one component
criteria.actionability: Folder structure is viable and buildable; shared files are annotated with owning component and consumers

design-delta.upstream: specs/design/ (the living baseline prior to this amendment) + specs/design/constitution.md + specs/brownfield/wiki/ (committed DeepWiki) + specs/brownfield/code-graph.json
design-delta.hard_gate: specs/reviews/design-grounding.json (trace-check.js over this sprint's design-traces.json vs its story-traces.json) must have pass=true — any net_new or dropped component is an automatic FAIL. specs/reviews/contract-drift-verdict.json (contract-drift-gate.js over api-contracts.schema.json) must not be verdict=breaking unless the amendment's Breaking Changes section names and justifies every broken endpoint. Any invariant in specs/design/constitution.md under '## Invariants' that this amendment violates is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average — quote the violated invariant verbatim in the finding.

criteria.completeness: The amendment narrative (specs/design/amendments/<amendment-id>.md) covers every story in this sprint/change: what it changes in the existing architecture, options considered, the recommendation, and per-component impact. Every changed specs/design/ file has a corresponding narrative section.
criteria.traceability: Every design change traces to a story/BR-n (anchored to specs/reviews/design-grounding.json when present). Every edit cites a specific committed DeepWiki page/symbol or code-graph node for the code it touches — an edit with no such citation is an orphan change.
criteria.specificity: Each edit names the existing module/seam/layer it extends, consistent with specs/brownfield/code-graph.json. Breaking changes are enumerated with the exact endpoint/field and a concrete justification, not a general note.
criteria.consistency: No edit introduces a new parallel structure where an existing seam already fits (reject and name the seam it should have extended instead). The amendment does not contradict any specs/design/constitution.md invariant.
criteria.actionability: The living specs/design/ artifacts (architecture.md, schemas, component-map.md) are updated non-destructively and are immediately buildable by /auto — no dangling references to the pre-amendment shape remain.

brownfield.hard_gate: specs/brownfield/code-graph.json must exist, parse as JSON, and have a non-empty nodes array with meta.producer set; and all five prose maps (codebase-map.md, architecture-map.md, test-map.md, risk-map.md, change-strategy.md) must exist and be non-empty. A missing or empty graph or map is an automatic FAIL regardless of the weighted average — prose claims about a codebase with no deterministic graph behind them are unverifiable.

criteria.completeness: All source directories and modules appear in the architecture map; no discovered code left unmapped
criteria.traceability: Every architectural claim cites a specific graph edge, file path, or test name as evidence
criteria.specificity: Risk map entries cite specific file paths and quantified metrics (coupling score, test coverage %, cyclomatic complexity)
criteria.consistency: Coupling numbers and dependency counts match values in code-graph.json; no contradictions between sections
criteria.actionability: Concrete lane recommendation (vibe/change/refactor/build) provided per area with justification

criteria.completeness: >= 3 diverse seam candidates identified across different parts of the codebase
criteria.traceability: Every candidate cites real file paths, interface names, and function signatures from the codebase
criteria.specificity: All 3 scoring axes evaluated per candidate: observability (0-10), funnel impact (0-10), read/write asymmetry (0-10)
criteria.consistency: Candidates are independent — cutting one seam does not require or preclude cutting another
criteria.actionability: Clear top recommendation with estimated effort, risk level, and expected benefit

criteria.completeness: Every service in architecture has a Dockerfile and a docker-compose entry
criteria.traceability: Docker Compose service names match component names from architecture document
criteria.specificity: YAML is syntactically valid; base images use pinned tags (no :latest); health checks defined for every service
criteria.consistency: No port conflicts between services; environment variable names are consistent across compose and application config
criteria.actionability: init.sh bootstraps all services end-to-end; .env.example contains every required variable with placeholder values
